pr.title: Split out known pl files from pl_0371
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Large assembly-only organizational split: most of the contents previously living in asm/melee/pl/pl_0371.s were moved into known player-related assembly files plbonus.s, plbonuslib.s, pl_040D.s, and pltrick.s, with obj_files.mk updated so the new split objects participate in the build. The diff indicates this was not a decompilation-to-C change but a source-layout cleanup that made known pl module boundaries explicit while preserving the remaining pl_0371.s content.
what_changed: Removed roughly 11,926 lines from asm/melee/pl/pl_0371.s, leaving only a small amount of retained content plus a symbol-label adjustment near pl_8003759C.
Created asm/melee/pl/plbonus.s with roughly 5,697 lines of assembly split out from the old combined pl_0371.s region.
Created asm/melee/pl/plbonuslib.s with roughly 4,314 lines of assembly split out from pl_0371.s.
Created asm/melee/pl/pl_040D.s with roughly 1,085 lines of assembly split out from pl_0371.s.
Created asm/melee/pl/pltrick.s with roughly 877 lines of assembly split out from pl_0371.s.
Updated obj_files.mk with four added entries so the new assembly files are built as separate objects.
Promoted the local label .L_8003759C to a global symbol named pl_8003759C in pl_0371.s, likely because the split boundary required the symbol to be externally visible.
smart_moves: Split a very large monolithic assembly file along known module names instead of waiting for full C decompilation, improving navigation and future ownership of the pl codebase.
Kept the change focused on file boundaries and build registration, minimizing semantic risk despite the very large diff.
Updated obj_files.mk in the same PR as the assembly split, avoiding a common failure mode where newly split asm files exist but are not linked in the build.

Made pl_8003759C global when it remained in pl_0371.s, which is the kind of symbol-visibility fix needed when code that previously shared one assembly file is separated into different compilation units.
decomp_lessons: When a monolithic asm file contains already-known source-file regions, splitting those regions into named asm files is useful preparatory work for later decompilation.
Assembly splits may require converting formerly local .L_ labels into global symbols if other split files need to branch to or reference them across object boundaries.
A successful split should include the build-system object list update in the same patch; otherwise the repository can silently lose code from the link.
Large asm moves are best treated as organizational changes unless the diff shows actual instruction edits; here the evidence primarily supports relocation and symbol/export cleanup.
Known file names such as plbonus, plbonuslib, pltrick, and pl_040D can encode module boundaries even before functions are decompiled into C.
naming_conventions: Known player module assembly files live under asm/melee/pl/ and use names such as plbonus.s, plbonuslib.s, pltrick.s, and pl_040D.s.
Address-based fallback symbols in player code use the pl_800xxxxx form for globals, as shown by pl_8003759C.
Local assembly labels use the .L_800xxxxx form and may need to become global pl_800xxxxx labels when crossing file/object boundaries.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Preserve raw assembly while changing only file placement to maintain matching behavior during an organizational split.
Use .global plus an address-derived symbol name when a previously local label must be referenced from another split assembly object.
Register each newly split assembly file in obj_files.mk so the object layout and link inputs reflect the new source-file boundaries.

Treat asserts, branch labels, and external calls such as __assert, Player_GetTotalAttackCountPtr, and ifMagnify_802FB6E8 as relocation-sensitive evidence when deciding split boundaries, but the provided excerpt does not prove these functions were edited semantically.
searchable_terms: Split out known pl files from pl_0371
follow_up_queries: Check the new plbonus.s, plbonuslib.s, pl_040D.s, and pltrick.s files for exact function ranges and remaining global/local label dependencies.
Look for later PRs that decompile plbonus, plbonuslib, pltrick, or pl_040D after this split prepared the assembly boundaries.
Verify whether pl_8003759C is referenced from one of the newly split files, explaining why it was promoted from .L_8003759C to a global symbol.
